artPrompt: A dramatic moment of a goblin snatching a pretzel from a tipped vendor cart in a rain-slick alley, the cart's awning dissolving into smoky-brown wash, a small apologetic token glinting in the amber lamplight below. Cigar smoke and rain streak the wide frame, the theft caught mid-grab, all gesture and one-sided friendliness. Intimate criminal menace, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Snack Cart Shakedown
text:
Take the table snack. The player who had it gains 2 life as an apology, and you create a Favor token. Real friendly. Real one-sided.
If an opponent already owes you a Favor, also draw a card.
flavorText: "That's a nice pretzel cart. Whateverville's got a lot of weather. Be a shame if it rained on a cart."
